title: ischaemum colladoa
intro: Ischaemum colladoa, also known as Indian staff, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever and inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, treating digestive issues, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Ischaemum colladoa is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being made into capsules or tinctures for its medicinal uses.
